response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source-code work, and deep embedded networking/security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching) — none of which appear on the resume. His career centers on web and full-stack development in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang; his C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015). The hard language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and systems domains
Genuine systems-level skills: low-latency DLang services with async I/O, plus some C/C++ IDE component work
Strong engineering culture: TDD, testing-tool authorship, CI/CD, and knowledge sharing through workshops
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a non-negotiable native-level requirement for this role
No evidence of Linux kernel analysis/modification, embedded firmware, or LAN-switch/networking experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP, NETCONF, L2/L3)
C programming experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; recent work is entirely web/full-stack

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel background
